tasks.title: Specify to-intelligence integration repair chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured chapter proposal that turns the rejected provider adapter work into an executable repair chapter without duplicating active task 1301.
tasks.context: Tasks 1301-1305 were rejected because provider registration is readiness-visible but not reachable from the normal Narada-native carrier task execution path. This task produces the next chapter specification or amendment handoff for that repair.
required_work: Inspect review findings on tasks 1301-1305 and the current state of task 1301 before proposing any new build tasks.
Define the smallest ordered repair chapter needed to wire provider selection into the governed work-loop or task-handoff path.
Include tasks for preserving provider output as inert handoff evidence and for proving the fixture fallback still works.
Represent the result as structured chapter input or an explicit no-new-chapter rationale if the active 1301-1305 tasks already cover the repair.
Submit the chapter proposal through a governed handoff for Architect commissioning.
non_goals: Do not create duplicate build tasks that race active task 1301.
Do not bypass provider capability references or store raw provider secrets.
Do not perform live provider calls as part of chapter specification.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal names the exact current gap from the rejected reviews.
The proposed tasks are ordered and do not duplicate active claimed work.
The proposal preserves Intelligence-Authority Separation and inert output admission.
The handoff is ready for chapter commission or explicitly explains why no new chapter should be created.

tasks.title: Specify to-data adapter foundation chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for the Narada-native carrier to-data side: bounded read access to Narada and local Site data without authority mutation.
tasks.context: The carrier can be factored as to-data, to-intelligence, and an orchestration wrapper. The to-data side should provide governed reads of task packets, inbox/work-next, readiness, evidence, and bounded local files.
required_work: Inspect existing task-handoff, readiness, adapter-registration, and supervisor read surfaces.
Define a to-data interface for bounded task, inbox, work-next, readiness, evidence, and file read packets.
Specify capability and attribution requirements for each read family.
Include tests proving no raw secret values, unbounded transcripts, or authority-bearing mutations are recorded.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered implementation and verification tasks.
non_goals: Do not grant task, inbox, outbox, command, publication, or repository mutation authority to to-data adapters.
Do not make direct SQLite reads the normal to-data interface.
Do not collapse local file readability into Narada authority.
acceptance_criteria: The chapter proposal defines a clear to-data contract and adapter set.
Every read surface has capability, attribution, and bounded evidence posture.
Mutation authority remains outside the carrier.
The proposed tasks are ready for governed chapter commission.

tasks.title: Specify carrier orchestration wrapper chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for composing to-data and to-intelligence adapters inside a supervised Narada-native carrier session.
tasks.context: A usable carrier needs a wrapper that obtains bounded data, invokes configured intelligence, and emits inert handoff drafts without owning task or effect authority.
required_work: Inspect current work-loop, task-handoff, supervisor, and reconstruction surfaces.
Define orchestration boundaries between to-data reads, to-intelligence invocation, and canonical handoff emission.
Specify fallback behavior for missing data adapters, missing provider configuration, refusal, timeout, and malformed outputs.
Include tests proving orchestration emits bounded handoff drafts and preserves fixture fallback.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered build tasks.
non_goals: Do not let the wrapper directly close, report, publish, command, or mutate inbox state.
Do not make provider text a Narada decision.
Do not require live network providers in normal tests.
acceptance_criteria: The proposed chapter cleanly composes to-data and to-intelligence through a wrapper.
The wrapper has explicit refusal and fallback behavior.
All outputs remain inert until canonical admission.
The chapter is ready for governed commission.

tasks.title: Specify capability and consent binding chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for binding provider capabilities, data-read capabilities, and consent records to Narada-native carrier sessions.
tasks.context: Carrier adapters should receive references and projected grants, not raw secrets or implicit authority. Capability and consent binding needs a coherent chapter before live use.
required_work: Inspect current adapter registration and capability posture code.
Define how provider capability refs, data-read capability refs, and consent records are projected into a session.
Specify secret redaction, stale grant, revoked grant, and missing consent behavior.
Include reconstruction and doctor requirements for configured, blocked, revoked, and fixture-only states.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered implementation and verification tasks.
non_goals: Do not implement a raw secret store in the carrier.
Do not infer consent from the presence of config files.
Do not grant mutation capabilities through this chapter.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal separates credential references, capability grants, and consent posture.
The carrier receives only projected authority needed for bounded reads or provider invocation.
Revoked or missing grants produce bounded blocked posture.
The chapter is ready for governed commission.

tasks.title: Specify live supervised session chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for a real Narada-native supervised session lifecycle with start, heartbeat, interrupt, close, and reconstruction evidence.
tasks.context: The current supervised lifecycle is fixture-oriented. A live supervised session chapter should prove runtime handles and adapter reachability without granting Narada authority to the carrier.
required_work: Inspect supervisor lifecycle, doctor, readiness, and reconstruction code.
Define live session startup and heartbeat evidence that can report to-data and to-intelligence reachability.
Specify interrupt, close, failure, and reconstruction behavior.
Include tests for blocked, fixture-only, provider-backed, and live-running states.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered build tasks.
non_goals: Do not require a permanently running daemon to pass unit tests.
Do not store raw transcripts or raw provider outputs in supervisor evidence.
Do not let runtime liveness become task authority.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal defines live supervised session lifecycle evidence.
Doctor output distinguishes fixture-only, provider-backed, blocked, failed, and live-running states.
Session reconstruction works from durable bounded evidence.
The chapter is ready for governed commission.

tasks.title: Specify governed effect handoff chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for converting Narada-native carrier outputs into canonical inert task, inbox, command, outbox, or publication handoff intents.
tasks.context: The carrier may propose work but must not execute authority-bearing effects. Effect handoff needs canonical artifact shapes and admission boundaries.
required_work: Inspect current work-loop handoff, task report draft, command intent, inbox, outbox, and publication posture docs or code.
Define proposed action packet shapes for task reports, inbox submissions, command intents, outbox intents, and publication intents.
Specify admission commands or surfaces required after each handoff type.
Include tests proving direct mutation remains false and handoff artifacts are reconstructable.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered build tasks.
non_goals: Do not allow the carrier to execute shell commands, send outbound messages, close tasks, or publish repositories directly.
Do not treat model output as accepted evidence without review or admission.
Do not record raw transcripts in handoff artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal covers the canonical handoff families needed by Narada-native.
Each handoff family has an explicit admission boundary.
Direct mutation by the carrier remains structurally false.
The chapter is ready for governed commission.

tasks.title: Specify operator launch and doctor affordance chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for operator-facing Narada-native launch, readiness, and doctor affordances.
tasks.context: Operators need to see whether Narada-native is configured, blocked, fixture-only, provider-backed, or live without reading private implementation files.
required_work: Inspect existing agent-start, supervisor doctor, readiness, and operator-surface patterns.
Define launch and doctor commands or surfaced affordances for Narada-native sessions.
Specify bounded output fields for provider posture, data posture, consent posture, runtime posture, and latest evidence refs.
Include tests proving command output is bounded and does not expose raw secrets, prompts, or model output.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered build tasks.
non_goals: Do not make launch affordances an authority locus.
Do not expose raw provider configuration or credential values.
Do not require an operator UI rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal defines clear operator-facing launch and doctor surfaces.
Output distinguishes configured, blocked, fixture-only, provider-backed, and live states.
Evidence refs are bounded and reconstructable.
The chapter is ready for governed commission.

tasks.title: Specify end-to-end Narada-native proof chapter
tasks.goal: Create a structured build chapter for proving one Builder task can pass through Narada-native data read, intelligence invocation, inert handoff, and canonical admission.
tasks.context: After the component chapters, Narada-native needs an end-to-end proof that shows the carrier can do useful governed work without owning Narada authority.
required_work: Define prerequisites from the to-data, to-intelligence, orchestration, capability, session, handoff, and operator affordance chapters.
Specify one controlled Builder-task scenario for end-to-end proof.
Define evidence requirements for data read, provider invocation, handoff draft, canonical admission, review, and reconstruction.
Include negative tests proving the carrier cannot directly mutate task, inbox, command, outbox, or publication state.
Submit structured chapter input with ordered proof tasks.
non_goals: Do not run the proof before prerequisite chapters are accepted.
Do not use private credentials or live provider calls unless explicitly capability-granted for the proof.
Do not close the proof by transcript inspection alone.
acceptance_criteria: The proposal defines an end-to-end proof with explicit prerequisites.
The proof covers data read, intelligence invocation, inert handoff, canonical admission, and reconstruction.
Negative authority tests are included.
The chapter is ready for governed commission when prerequisites are satisfied.
